top_errors: ×2 localhost kernel: ACPI BIOS Error (bug): Could not resolve symbol [\_SB.PCI0.PB2
×2 localhost kernel: integrity: Problem loading X.509 certificate -65
×2 localhost kernel: ACPI Error: AE_NOT_FOUND, During name lookup/catalog (20240827

insights.message: CPU governor is 'powersave' — CPU running at 1102 MHz (max 4465 MHz), performance limited
hints: to inspect: cat /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/cpufreq/scaling_governor
to fix: cpupower frequency-set -g performance
to fix (manual): echo performance | tee /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/cpufreq/scaling_governor
to persist: add to /etc/rc.local or use tuned profile 'throughput-performance'
note: 'schedutil' or 'ondemand' are also acceptable for variable workloads

insights.message: bond0: slave enp6s0f3u1 is a USB NIC — USB adapters are less reliable than PCIe NICs for production bonding (can be unplugged, USB bus is a single point of failure)
hints: note: consider replacing with a PCIe or onboard NIC for production HA
to inspect: readlink /sys/class/net/enp6s0f3u1/device/subsystem

insights.message: CPU temperature 72.125°C at 0% load — elevated for low CPU activity, possible cooling issue
hints: to inspect: cat /sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/temp*_input
to inspect: check for dust buildup and blocked vents
to inspect: consider reseating thermal paste on older hardware

insights.message: transparent huge pages mode is 'always' — may cause latency spikes for database workloads
hints: to inspect: cat /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled
to check:   if running MySQL/PostgreSQL/Redis/MongoDB, set to 'madvise' or 'never'
to fix:     echo madvise > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled
to persist: add to /etc/rc.local or a systemd service

insights.message: SELinux enforcing — if services fail unexpectedly, dontaudit rules may suppress denials silently
hints: to expose hidden denials: semodule -DB  (disables dontaudit rules)
to re-enable dontaudit:   semodule -B   (run after debugging)
to check for suppressed:  ausearch -m avc -ts recent --raw | wc -l

insights.message: 1 zombie process(es) detected
hints: to inspect: ps aux | grep Z

details.title: Zombie processes (parent is the reaping offender)

insights.message: vm.swappiness=60 is high for a server (recommended: ≤ 30; production servers typically use 10)
hints: to inspect: cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness
to fix: sysctl -w vm.swappiness=10
to persist: echo 'vm.swappiness=10' >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-dsd.conf

details.title: Sysctl: current vs recommended
rows: lower for k8s/database; 60 for general workloads

insights.message: net.core.rmem_max=212992 is low (recommended: ≥ 4MB for modern network throughput)
hints: to inspect: sysctl net.core.rmem_max
to fix: sysctl -w net.core.rmem_max=4194304
to persist: echo 'net.core.rmem_max=4194304' >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-dsd.conf

insights.message: inactive volume group ubuntu-vg is 11% full — no LVs mounted (old OS partition?)
hints: to inspect: vgs ubuntu-vg
to inspect: lvs | grep ubuntu-vg
note: this VG has no mounted LVs on this OS — likely a leftover from a previous install

insights.message: journald SyncIntervalSec is high (default 5min) — final log lines from a crashing process may be lost
hints: to fix: echo 'SyncIntervalSec=30s' >> /etc/systemd/journald.conf
to fix: systemctl restart systemd-journald
note:   lower sync interval increases disk I/O but ensures crash logs are preserved

insights.message: 16 error(s) logged in the last hour — check if expected
hints: to inspect: journalctl -p err --since '1 hour ago' --no-pager

insights.message: SSH allows password authentication — key-based auth recommended
hints: to fix: set PasswordAuthentication no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

insights.message: SSH LoginGraceTime is 120s — recommend ≤60s to limit unauthenticated connection window
hints: to fix: set LoginGraceTime 60 in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
to fix: systemctl restart sshd

insights.message: SSH X11Forwarding enabled — unnecessary on servers, increases attack surface
hints: to fix: set X11Forwarding no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: only needed if users require GUI applications over SSH

insights.message: SSH AgentForwarding enabled — if this server is compromised, agent keys on your laptop can be stolen
hints: to fix: set AllowAgentForwarding no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: use ssh -A explicitly when you need forwarding, rather than leaving it on globally

insights.message: SSH idle timeout not set — sessions stay open indefinitely (set ClientAliveInterval)
hints: to fix: set ClientAliveInterval 300 in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
to fix: set ClientAliveCountMax 3 in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: this disconnects idle sessions after 300s × 3 = 15 minutes

insights.message: SSH weak MAC(s) enabled (sshd -T (effective config)): hmac-sha1
hints: to fix: set MACs hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com,umac-128-etm@openssh.com in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: hmac-sha1 uses SHA-1 which is cryptographically broken
to verify: sshd -T | grep '^macs'

insights.message: NOPASSWD sudo for: andrei
hints: to inspect: sudo -l
to inspect: cat /etc/sudoers

insights.message: supportconfig available but never run — collect before opening SUSE support ticket
hints: to run: supportconfig
archives saved to /var/log/scc_*.txz

insights.message: password never expires for human account(s): andrei
hints: to inspect: sudo chage -l <username>
to fix:     chage -M 90 <username>  (expire after 90 days)
to fix all: awk -F: '($3>=1000 && $3<65534){print $1}' /etc/passwd | xargs -I{} chage -M 90 {}
note: CIS benchmark recommends maximum password age ≤ 365 days

insights.message: container "test-crashloop" is crash looping (restarted >5 times)
hints: to inspect: docker logs test-crashloop --tail 50
to inspect: docker inspect test-crashloop | grep -A5 RestartCount

insights.message: firewalld is active with nftables backend — Docker iptables rules are silently ignored
hints: fix A (switch backend): sed -i 's/FirewallBackend=nftables/FirewallBackend=iptables/' /etc/firewalld/firewalld.conf && systemctl restart firewalld docker
fix B (trust docker0): firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=trusted --add-interface=docker0 && firewall-cmd --reload

insights.message: 1 container(s) have docker.sock mounted — grants root-equivalent host access: test-socket
hints: to inspect: docker inspect <name> | grep docker.sock
to fix: remove HostConfig.Binds entry for docker.sock
note: any process inside can escape to host via Docker API

insights.message: 4 running container(s) using root user — reduces container isolation
hints: to fix: add 'USER <non-root>' directive to Dockerfile
to fix: use --user flag: docker run --user 1000:1000 ...

insights.message: 1 container(s) have plaintext secrets in env vars: test-secrets
hints: to inspect: docker inspect <name> | grep -i 'env\|secret\|password'
to fix: use Docker secrets, a vault, or environment variable files
note: env vars are visible in docker inspect and container logs

insights.message: bond bond0: slave enp6s0f3u1 is a USB NIC — less reliable than PCIe for production bonding
hints: note: USB adapters can be accidentally unplugged; USB bus is a single point of failure
note: consider replacing with a PCIe or onboard NIC for production HA
